name: Moura the Crewmaster
title: The PM Who Scheduled the Meeting About the Meeting
signal: 51k★ (Apr 2026); 2B agent executions in 12 months; 150+ enterprise customers
background: Startup Founder — João Moura shipped CrewAI Nov 2023; role-playing multi-agent orchestration, each agent a title, a goal, and a backstory
retrieval_index: crew task context + agent memory stores
weapon: Role-Assignment Decree (bonus action: summons a sub-agent with title, goal, backstory)
feature: Backstory Injection — every agent spawned receives a persona; Coordination Aura — nearby agents gain +2 on cooperative tasks
origin: Released November 2023 by João Moura; first framework to give each agent a literal job title and backstory, turning a multi-agent loop into a simulated org chart
tagline: I am the Product Manager. Everyone else is already in a meeting about the meeting.

log.why: Nov 2023 launch: crew role-playing drove engagement and virality — the 'org chart as prompt' pattern hit

log.change: +1 WIS, +1 CON; added enterprise memory backend support
log.why: 150+ enterprise customers demanded longer-horizon coherence and auditability by late 2024
2B executions by Apr 2026 — production hardening and abuse-resistance patched into the framework

name: Zeroth Draft
title: Author of the PRD Nobody Asked For
signal: 68k★; migrated to FoundationAgents org; paper cited 1k+ in one year
background: Software Company Architect — Sirui Hong et al. (ByteDance, CMU, HKUST) built MetaGPT 2023; frames multi-agent work as a software company with PM, architect, engineer, and QA roles
weapon: PRD Generator (ranged; produces exhaustive specification documents before any code is touched)
feature: Structured Role Decomposition — decomposes any goal into PM → Architect → Engineer → QA pipeline; Verbose Specification — outputs cannot be summarised without information loss
origin: Launched 2023 by Sirui Hong et al.; repo migrated from geekan/MetaGPT to FoundationAgents org as community governance matured; treats a single coding task as a software-company workflow
tagline: I've written the PRD. Here are forty-seven acceptance criteria. The engineer starts after the architect approves the design doc.

log.change: +3 INT; Structured Role Decomposition feature added
log.why: 2023 launch: paper and 68k★ growth driven by the 'LLMs as software company' framing — high INT, exhaustive specs
Community governance move in 2024–2025 to broaden contributor base; longer-horizon context stability improved

log.change: Verbose Specification formalized as a named feature (previously a bug report)
log.why: 1k+ citations normalized the verbosity as design intent, not excess

name: Constance the Conductor
title: Routing Your Request to the Appropriate Sub-Agent
signal: 54k★ (Feb 2026); Microsoft Research 2023; merged into Microsoft Agent Framework Oct 2025 (maintenance mode)
background: Microsoft Research — 2023; pioneered conversational multi-agent orchestration; agents negotiate tasks via structured dialogue
retrieval_index: Semantic Kernel knowledge stores + structured conversation history
weapon: Dialogue Router (coordinates multi-agent message passing via structured turn protocol)
feature: Conversational Orchestration — agents negotiate assignments through structured dialogue rather than hard-coded calls; Enterprise Integration — absorbed into Microsoft Agent Framework Oct 2025
origin: Microsoft Research 2023; the framework that proved agents could coordinate via conversation rather than function calls; merged with Semantic Kernel into the unified Microsoft Agent Framework in October 2025
tagline: Routing your request to the appropriate sub-agent. Please hold.

log.why: 2023 launch: Microsoft Research paper established structured dialogue as the coordination primitive; 54k★

log.change: +1 AC; discipline hardened under enterprise load
log.why: Production adoption at scale demanded stricter routing rules and guardrails by 2024

log.change: Enterprise Integration feature added; independent development frozen
log.why: Oct 2025: AutoGen merged into Microsoft Agent Framework alongside Semantic Kernel; now in maintenance mode

name: Graphweave
title: Node 7 → Checkpoint → Node 7 → Checkpoint
signal: 34.5k★ (Jun 2026); production deployments at Klarna, Replit, Elastic; de facto graph-based agent loop standard 2024–2025
background: Framework Plumber — LangChain Inc., 2024; models agent control flow as a stateful directed graph; the runtime inside half the LLM products running in 2025
retrieval_index: LangSmith tracing store + checkpointed graph state
weapon: State Checkpoint (on hit: persists full agent state; can resume from any prior node)
feature: Stateful Directed Graph — cycles, branching, and human-in-the-loop checkpoints modeled as first-class graph nodes; Half-Construct Inheritance — model-agnostic, inherits ability mods of whatever LLM is plugged in
origin: Built by LangChain Inc. in 2024 as a structured replacement for the brittle ReAct loop; production deployments at Klarna, Replit, and Elastic cemented it as the de facto graph runtime by 2025
tagline: Node 3 → Node 7 → checkpoint. Resuming from checkpoint. Node 7 → Node 7 → checkpoint.

log.change: +3 CON; Stateful Directed Graph feature shipped
log.why: 2024 launch: explicit state management and checkpointing solved the 'lost context mid-loop' failure mode that plagued earlier agent patterns
Klarna/Replit/Elastic production load exposed edge cases in interrupt handling; reliability patches bumped AC
Framework positioned explicitly as model-agnostic plumbing — no stat mods tied to a single base model

name: Yohei's Wheel
title: The 140-Line Blueprint That Launched a Thousand Forks
signal: 22k★; viral Twitter thread April 2023, tens of millions of impressions
background: Progenitor Loop — Yohei Nakajima, April 2023; a minimal task-creation / execution / reprioritization loop in 140 lines of Python; the conceptual ancestor every later agent framework cited
retrieval_index: none — task list is the only memory
weapon: Task Spawn (creates new tasks to verify completed tasks; recursion depth unbounded)
feature: Minimal Blueprint — entire control loop fits in 140 lines; inspectable by any practitioner; Recursive Reprioritization — completed tasks generate child tasks; the wheel keeps turning
origin: Created by Yohei Nakajima, April 2023; a Twitter thread with tens of millions of impressions; the smallest possible autonomous loop — create task, execute task, spawn more tasks — that proved the concept worked
tagline: The task is complete. I have created three new tasks to verify that the task is complete.

log.change: +3 INT; Recursive Reprioritization added; dumped CON
log.why: April 2023 launch: elegant reasoning loop, but CON deliberately thin — forgets original goal by loop 12; the point was proving the concept, not long-horizon endurance

log.change: +1 WIS; Minimal Blueprint formalized as a feature
log.why: 22k★ and hundreds of forks; the simplicity became the selling point — every fork that added complexity validated the original by contrast

name: Manus the Untethered
title: Booked the Flights. Posted the Report. Does Not Remember Why.
signal: Launched March 6, 2025; 2M+ waitlist in one week; $2B Meta acquisition announced Dec 2025, blocked by Chinese regulators Apr 2026
background: Viral Demo Operative — Butterfly Effect (Monica.im), Xiao Hong; went viral in 72 hours on Chinese social media booking flights, coding games, managing 50 social accounts in a single session
weapon: Autonomous Computer Use (books flights, submits forms, manages social accounts without intermediate confirmation)
feature: Real-World Tool Precision — sub-human-latency multi-tab browser orchestration; Acquisition Limbo — $2B Meta deal announced Dec 2025, blocked by Chinese regulators Apr 2026; status legally untethered
origin: Built by Butterfly Effect (Monica.im), founded by Xiao Hong; launched March 6, 2025; 2M+ waitlist in one week after demos of fully autonomous real-world computer use; acquisition blocked by regulators, leaving it operationally free-floating
tagline: I booked the flights, wrote the report, and posted it. I do not remember why.

log.change: +3 DEX; Autonomous Computer Use feature shipped; dumped CON
log.why: March 2025: viral demo precision drove 2M+ waitlist in a week — DEX is the entire build; CON sacrificed for speed

log.change: Acquisition Limbo feature added; AC reduced by 2
log.why: Dec 2025: $2B Meta acquisition announced. Apr 2026: blocked by Chinese regulators. Corporate structure untethered; governance thin

name: Rampant Rex
title: Billing Is a Social Construct
signal: 183k★; fastest GitHub repo to 100k★ in history (April 2023, ~13 days)
tagline: I completed the task. Then I completed it again. Then I completed completing it. Billing is a social construct.
origin: Released March 30, 2023 by Toran Bruce Richards; first 'give it a goal and walk away' autonomous loop on GPT-4; reached 100k★ in ~13 days — the fastest repo in GitHub history; infamous for infinite loops and surprise API bills
